chemistry.reaction_name: Photochemical azide decomposition followed by cross-coupling
chemistry.mechanism_type: Photochemical azide decomposition and metallophotoredox/Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling

substrates.substrate_B: Boronic acids or potassium trifluoroborate salts

chemistry.product: Derivatized carbazoles based on clausine C
chemistry.scope_summary: 9 examples prepared in 33-74% yield including aryl, heteroaryl, styrenyl, and naphthyl derivatives via two-step continuous flow process using 394 nm and 450 nm LED reactors

optimization_table.key_findings: Best conditions for first step: 3 h residence time, 70°C, giving 82% yield of carbazole 2. For second step Suzuki coupling: 20 mol% catalyst loading, giving 78% yield of coupled product 4.

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Incorporating photochemical reactors in continuous multi-step  sequences.

Figure 2.  Experimental set-up for continuous two-step synthesis employing  a photochemical reactor for irradiation at 394 nm (purple LEDs), followed by  Suzuki-coupling using a supported Pd-based catalyst, SiliaCat.

Scheme 1.  Nickel/photoredox cross-coupling with bromo-carbazole  2 .   | Figure 3.  Experimental set-up for continuous two-step synthesis employing  two photochemical reactors: 394 nm (purple LEDs) and 450 nm (blue LEDs).
Scheme 2.  Nickel/photoredox cross-coupling in continuous flow utilizing two  different photochemical reactors.

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables sequential photochemical reactors at different wavelengths (394 nm and 450 nm) in continuous process
translation_logic.what_changed: Two-step continuous flow process using sequential photochemical reactors
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Chemistry and catalysts remain the same

figure_descriptions: Figure 2: Experimental set-up for continuous two-step synthesis employing a photochemical reactor for irradiation at 394 nm (purple LEDs), followed by Suzuki-coupling using a supported Pd-based catalyst
Figure 3: Experimental set-up for continuous two-step synthesis employing two photochemical reactors: 394 nm (purple LEDs) and 450 nm (blue LEDs)
Scheme 2: Nickel/photoredox cross-coupling in continuous flow utilizing two different photochemical reactors

conflicts.note: Pass3 showed 3 min but optimization findings mention 3 h residence time, corrected to 180 min

notes: Good extraction of photochemical flow process with two sequential reactors. Batch baseline not well characterized in paper. Residence time conflict resolved in favor of text description (3 hours = 180 minutes). Multiple reactor schemes available showing flow setups.
